title: Unexpected bash script failure during build
body:
### Issue Description

Building container fails due to the bash script error - `Bad substitution` if the Podman is being used. With the Docker, same Dockerfile and script, everything works. The same base image is used.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create `Dockerfile` and `script.sh` as specified below
2. Execute `podman build --no-cache -t test .`
3. Execute `docker build --no-cache -t test .`

`Dockerfile`
```
FROM debian:11-slim

# this works
# RUN printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\nSCRIPT_DIR="$(cd $(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}") && pwd)"' >> script.sh

COPY script.sh script.sh
RUN chmod +x script.sh && ./script.sh
    
ENTRYPOINT [] 
```

`script.sh`
```
#!/usr/bin/env bash

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd $(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}") && pwd)"
```

### Describe the results you received

```
STEP 3/4: RUN chmod +x script.sh && ./script.sh
./script.sh: 4: Bad substitution
--> 82644cf4d449
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
STEP 3/4: RUN chmod +x script.sh && ./script.sh
--> 82644cf4d449
```

comments.body: i just tried this with main branch and it is not reproducing.
fwiw, i could not trip it from 4.6 either. i see this is coreos, lemme see about that too
unable to replicate there as well.  are you doing something like `podman build -t foo .` ?  Anything funny about your setup?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
